headline: Parliament sat for only 67 days in 2025 — lowest in ASEAN
context: Dewan Rakyat sat for only 67 days in 2025, the fewest among ASEAN parliamentary democracies and below the 80-day minimum recommended by the Commonwealth Parliamentary Association.

cards.big: Parliament met just 67 days last year — MPs worked fewer days than school students
cards.sub: Lowest sitting days among ASEAN democracies and below Commonwealth minimums

cards.big: Standing Order 11(1) gives PM sole power to determine sitting calendar
cards.sub: Opposition has no mechanism to convene emergency parliamentary sessions

cards.big: Each MP costs taxpayers RM1.2 million annually in salary and allowances
cards.sub: At 67 days, that's RM17,910 per sitting day per MP — for often half-empty chambers

cards.big: Indonesia's DPR sat 142 days and Philippines Congress sat 118 days in 2025
cards.sub: Regional peers demonstrate that more sitting days are operationally feasible

cards.big: A parliament that barely meets is a parliament that barely governs — the executive fills the vacuum
Should minimum parliamentary sitting days be constitutionally mandated at 100 per year?
